notes: Current public development-line manifest for the 4.1.3.8 task-execution quality hardening work after the 4.1.3.7 AgentExecution-backed AgentTask release. The target is framework-level bug fixes and structural cleanup: make task planning capability-aware, enforce bounded-step scope through structured policy/evidence contracts, improve verification and replan quality across task levels, remove strong-instruction or business-specific special-case fixes from framework paths, and project AgentExecution flat/TaskBoard process stream items to RuntimeEvent for DevTools observation.

runtime_event_ownership.plugin_contract: plugins return observations/errors/decisions; core maps them to official RuntimeEvent records

runtime_naming.agent_execution: run_kind for one AgentExecution-owned Agent run
runtime_naming.attempt_index: model-request retry attempt metadata; not an AgentExecution counter

runtime_control.model_request_telemetry_contract: Existing model RuntimeEvents may carry payload.model_request_telemetry observation facts for response_id, attempt_index, run ids, provider/model, request_url, duration, raw usage, usage_summary provider-token normalization, estimated input/output character lengths, side_channel, and normalized errors. Terminal model.status events may carry estimated input/output character lengths without exposing raw request payloads. Telemetry dedupe is observation-only and must not drive routing, retry, verification, quality judgment, planner input, or prompt content.
runtime_control.model_request_usage_consumption: DevTools consumes payload.model_request_telemetry.usage_summary first, may fall back to provider usage metadata, displays single model-request usage and selected-run descendant aggregate usage, shows unavailable provider token counts as NaN, and treats estimated input/output character lengths as diagnostics only.
runtime_control.agent_task_action_observation_contract: AgentTask observation projects normalized agent_task.action.started, agent_task.action.completed, and agent_task.action.failed stream events from bounded execution action_logs. Payloads carry safe input summaries, output summaries, refs, diagnostics, work-unit ownership, projection_source=execution_meta.action_logs, and posthoc_projection=true when reconstructed from recorded logs. Recovered success and partial_success Action records project as completed observations; failed observations are reserved for failed, blocked, timed-out, or unrecovered error records. These events are observation-only and must not drive routing, verification, quality judgment, semantic relevance decisions, or task completion acceptance.
runtime_control.model_request_result_stream_status_contr: ModelRequestResult reserves $status for completed, failed, and cancelled ModelRequest attempt outcomes. failed/retry=true invalidates provisional stream output before a replacement attempt; plain delta streams emit the standalone <$retry>{reason}</$retry> marker at that replay boundary, while $status remains the structured fact source. AgentExecution projects $status and response/run lineage in its structured process stream. model.status RuntimeEvents are observation-only. OpenAICompatible after-output transport replay is explicit through request_retry.after_output and remains disabled by default.

runtime_capability_contract.skill_capability_needs: SkillsExecutor records selected Skill capability needs in SkillExecutionPlan.capability_needs from SKILL.md guidance, resource indexes, public compatibility, public metadata, and optional model-assisted inference.
runtime_capability_contract.skill_context_pack_contract: SkillsExecutor exposes build_context_pack(...), async_build_context_pack(...), and task_dag_resolver() for custom planners and TaskDAG skill nodes. The agently.skills.context_pack.v1 payload includes SKILL.md guidance excerpts, task-relevant references/examples/assets, citations, diagnostics, fail-closed public lookup, and policy-gated script Action candidates without executing bundled scripts.
runtime_capability_contract.search_provider: Framework Search uses the ddgs package by default, keeps backend configurable through host policy, supports host-approved ddgs refresh before search runs, treats no-result search as a successful empty result, and falls back through configured/default ddgs backends when a selected backend returns no usable parsed result.
runtime_capability_contract.workspace_file_action_owner: Workspace owns file action roots and path boundaries; SkillsExecutor prefers workspace.enable_file_actions(agent, ...) before falling back to agent.enable_workspace_file_actions(...).
runtime_capability_contract.policy_approval_surface: Skills capability approval uses the framework-wide PolicyApproval handler surface: input_timeout_fail by default, fail_closed for pending diagnostics/TriggerFlow gates, auto_approve for tests, input, or registered service-specific handlers.
runtime_capability_contract.skills_abort_diagnostics: SkillsExecutor emits skills.execution.aborted on cooperative cancellation or framework execution failure with reason, strategy, effort, elapsed_seconds, and the last active runtime event when available. Built-in react/staged strategies emit skills.execution.budget_exhausted when step_budget stops or truncates work. Host wall-clock and no-progress limits remain host policy unless cancellation reaches the Skills runtime.

blocks.lifecycle_contract: Blocks is the internal lowering bridge from AgentTask ExecutionPlan / PlanBlock instances and validated TaskDAG nodes to TriggerFlow-backed ExecutionBlockGraph. It is not a second task lifecycle, does not grant capabilities, does not accept terminal task completion, and does not replace TaskDAG validation or TriggerFlow dispatch.
blocks.planner_contract: PlanBlock is a planner-visible specification over existing capability sources with explicit input, output, capability, evidence, and runtime binding contracts. PlanBlock selection is evidence of need, not permission.
blocks.runtime_contract: ExecutionBlock is trusted runtime code lowered to one TriggerFlow chunk or a fixed chunk/signal group. PlanBlock and ExecutionBlock registries validate known kinds, trusted runtime bindings, signal contracts, and resource/capability requirements; compile fails closed for missing plan edges, denied capabilities, or pending capabilities without a matching approval_wait. Handler-backed blocks require runtime handlers; workspace_operation requires a Workspace resource, supports bounded Workspace SQLite/FTS search, bounded Workspace file search with small bounded match context, and read_bounded facts, and separates locator_ref from evidence_snippet without judging semantic usefulness; approval_wait records waiting evidence while using PolicyApproval and TriggerFlow pause/resume; external_wait records waiting evidence while using TriggerFlow pause/resume.
blocks.skills_contract: Skills are progressive context/capability packages. SkillsExecutor exposes capability_adapter(), discover_skill_capabilities(...), and activate_skill(...) so Skill activation can feed PlanBlock selection and EvidenceEnvelope skill_context records without executing Skill resources or proving side effects. agent.run_skills_task(...) remains a compatibility facade that builds skill_activation plus concrete strategy PlanBlocks, lowers single_shot to a model_request ExecutionBlock, lowers runtime_chain/staged/react/custom labels to flow_segment ExecutionBlocks, and records Blocks plan/evidence metadata in SkillExecution.close_snapshot['blocks'].
blocks.task_dag_contract: TaskDAGExecutor.compile_blocks(...) and async_run_blocks(...) validate TaskDAG data under TaskDAG ownership, lower validated DAG nodes through Blocks, run on TriggerFlow, and map TaskDAG semantic outputs through ResultAdapter and EvidenceEnvelope.
blocks.agent_task_loop_contract: AgentTask bounded execution steps run through ExecutionPlan -> Blocks compile -> TriggerFlow execution -> EvidenceEnvelope/ResultAdapter metadata. Direct bounded steps lower as agent_step blocks. Flat and TaskBoard are coordination strategies, not separate execution carriers: both emit internal work-unit intent and receive carrier result metadata, while TaskBoard remains the owner of board schedule, dependency state, patch validation, continuation scheduling, and finalization. Flat scoped_retrieval.query_groups and TaskBoard source_ref_policy.scoped_retrieval_policy share the same work-unit retrieval contract: planner/control models may propose query groups with search_surface values workspace_index, workspace_files, or workspace_index_and_files; shared BlockCarrier lowers those query groups into pre-step Blocks workspace_operation.search facts and injects compact model-hot evidence_ledger plus compatibility scoped_retrieval_results into the downstream bounded agent_step or TaskBoard card while full SHA/byte/backend provenance stays in raw Workspace/Blocks evidence. EvidenceEnvelope.evidence_items is the canonical internal grounding ledger for Flat synthesis, TaskBoard card/final synthesis, verifier prompts, host guards, and artifact acceptance locators; each item preserves id, kind, status(ok|failed|empty), body_state(full|bounded|truncated|ref_only), provenance, and view-local cite_as handles, while failed/empty items support only unavailable or missing-data claims and ref_only items support only discovery/ref-pointer claims until readback evidence exists. Workspace search_files and Blocks workspace_operation return factual locator_ref/evidence_snippet records only with truncated bounded-snippet facts, Workspace search_files treats pattern='**' as recursive file search under the scoped path and uses rg as a grep-style engine when available, record collections belong in filters.collection, exact record kinds may use filters.kind, singleton structural filter lists are normalized to scalar values, and Workspace search accepts structural id/path/scope/meta filters for scoped retrieval. TaskBoard readback cards can read both Action artifact_refs and trusted Workspace file/content refs through bounded cold readbacks; Workspace artifact write/readback, acceptance-locator, and targeted artifact readbacks are recorded as ledger items before verification; acceptance locators are produced after real Workspace write/readback from actual file headings, offsets, line ranges, fingerprints, manifests, TaskBoard criteria, and optional acceptance_points intent; content/content_preview/text/excerpt/snippet fields are bounded previews for visible content only, and TaskBoard final verification carries board source_refs with preserved content_state boundaries; framework-synthesized readback/continuation patches scope readback to direct dependencies plus upstream evidence cards and do not recursively synthesize another readback chain from generated continuation cards. Structured target_refs are split by kind: external HTTP/HTTPS refs become Action evidence work, while Workspace/content paths and retained-note refs become direct bounded Workspace readback cards. Independent verifier requests are reserved for terminal acceptance, evidence/artifact boundary audit, contradictions, high-risk review, or cases where no downstream consumer remains; ordinary intermediate Flat work units with non-empty remaining_work default to deferring terminal verification so the next Flat iteration consumes their evidence, ready_for_final_verification=false may make the same intent explicit, explicit ready_for_final_verification=true hands the result to terminal/blocking/risk verification, and ordinary TaskBoard cards let downstream consumer cards decide whether evidence is sufficient and return structured continuation/readback intent when it is not; blocked scoped-retrieval cards without explicit next action synthesize expanded evidence and continuation cards instead of relying on a terminal verifier to repair intermediate evidence. Structured outputs may include evidence_use claim bindings and optional file-backed acceptance_points intent; deterministic host guards reconcile visible cite_as, producer-declared structural aliases, path, record_id, source_url, artifact, action id, and action/ref aliases to canonical ledger ids, reject ambiguous or unresolved ids, reject failed/empty positive support, and reject ref_only content support before verifier judgment without parsing or rewriting prose. Binding-only repair is targeted to evidence_use and does not regenerate the whole final deliverable. Failed terminal Flat verification carries compact repair_context into the next Flat work unit and Workspace artifact draft request when present, including acceptance deltas, repair constraints, next-step requirements, and exact evidence anchors without restoring cold provenance to model-hot prompts. TaskDAG is no longer an AgentTask bounded-step strategy; model-proposed dynamic_task/execution_dag shapes and effort.execution.step_plan='dag' are degraded to direct execution with diagnostics. TaskDAGExecutor.compile_blocks(...) remains the independent DAG-to-Blocks bridge for manually configured or visual-automation orchestration. Structured ReplanSignal evidence cancels only affected downstream ExecutionBlocks, blocks premature acceptance, and feeds the next AgentTask replan decision. Natural-language progress is an opt-in side channel: template progress emits complete progress items without model usage, while progress_model_key emits progress_delta events as streamed language output before the final progress item; progress_language can be set per execution or through agent_task.progress.language. Selected high-value AgentTask internal structured outputs may include short process fields such as task_intent, turn_intent, card_intent, decision_basis, self_check, short_summary, verification_summary, criterion_checks, repair_summary, and progress_message; these fields are process_summary records only, are not EvidenceEnvelope evidence, and progress_message is the only field projected as agent_task.process.progress text. get_async_generator(type="delta") is the public text-increment stream and projects selected process events (template progress, structured progress_message, action observations, snapshots, heartbeats, phases, retry markers, and terminal task results) into paragraph text; get_async_generator(type="instant") keeps the original structured event payloads. Artifact writers consume structured $status when available; if they intentionally consume plain delta for long natural-language bodies, they must treat <$retry>...</$retry> as a public replay boundary at the consumer side instead of forcing the body through .output() only to obtain instant fields.
blocks.agent_task_artifact_stream_contract: Long or prose-heavy artifact bodies may be streamed as natural text without .output(). AgentTask artifact writers should consume structured AgentExecution or ModelRequest status facts for retry/reset when available; when the chosen source is plain public delta, <$retry>...</$retry> is a consumer-side replay boundary and must not be written, cleaned into, or transported as artifact content. Trusted long Workspace artifact verification consumes acceptance-locator ledger items produced after Workspace readback, then bounded targeted_readbacks from locator line/offset anchors; declared output-contract sections and generic source/risk/reference/coverage anchors remain fallback while full artifact bodies stay in cold Workspace evidence.

action_runtime.partial_success_contract: Action results with status=partial_success keep success=True/ok=True and carry diagnostics for degraded providers or fallback recovery. ActionFlow treats partial_success as continuable evidence, not action.failed.
action_runtime.search_partial_success: Built-in Search returns partial_success when earlier ddgs backends fail or return empty parsed results but a later fallback backend returns usable results.
action_runtime.bash_sandbox_model_description: Model-visible bash sandbox action descriptions include the allowed command prefixes, allowed working-directory roots, timeout, output preview limit, and guidance that shell is for tests, builds, git inspection, and read-only diagnostics while Workspace file actions own file IO. agent.enable_shell(...) uses a small safe command profile when commands is omitted, and oversized stdout/stderr streams are persisted under artifacts/shell/ when a Workspace root is available.
action_runtime.coding_workspace_actions_contract: agent.enable_coding_agent_actions(...) exposes Workspace-owned read_file, glob_files, grep_files, edit_file, apply_patch, and guarded write_file actions over the current Workspace file root. edit_file supports expected_sha256 stale guards, apply_patch applies unified diffs inside the Workspace root and can require expected_files, and write_file in coding-agent mode requires prior read state or an expected hash unless the host disables that guard.
action_runtime.execution_resource_metadata_redaction: Action metadata returned by get_action_info()/get_action_list() redacts execution resource env values while preserving env key names; raw env values remain available only to the execution resource provider path. Renamed from ExecutionEnvironment to ExecutionResource in the 4.1.3.8 Workspace/ActionRuntime boundary refactor.
action_runtime.action_loop_timeout_contract: The timeout argument passed to get_action_result(...)/async_get_action_result(...) bounds the full ActionFlow lifecycle, including structured planning and native tool-call selection, and raises RuntimeStageStallError(stage="action_loop_close") when the loop cannot finish before the deadline.
action_runtime.explicit_action_loop_reply_contract: After get_action_result(..., store_for_reply=True) runs an explicit action loop, the prompt records action_results even when no action records were produced so later response materialization does not re-enter ActionRuntime for the same prompt.
action_runtime.action_evidence_refs_contract: Instruction-heavy or large Action records use model_digest as the hot-path model-visible summary and preserve full redacted inputs/outputs behind artifact_refs. Explicit artifacts/artifact_refs returned by an Action are normalized into the same artifact_refs channel even for small outputs; MCP resource/content blocks and structured artifact_refs/artifacts/file_refs are preserved as declared artifact metadata, while undeclared MCP file writes are not inferred by directory scanning. Artifact refs include role, media type, bytes, preview_size, sha256, truncation flags, and availability fields. Workspace file_refs returned by Action data are surfaced on ActionResult/model_digest. When a digest is still too large for later planning or reply hot paths, ActionRuntime compacts the model-visible digest again, replaces duplicate data/model_digest fields with same_as=result pointers, omits artifact preview bodies from hot-path artifact_refs, and keeps read_action_artifact available for explicit scoped readback. max_output_bytes records diagnostics and preserves full output for artifact finalization instead of destructively replacing the only data/result value.
action_runtime.model_input_safety_contract: Model-planned Action commands from structured_plan and native_tool_calls are filtered at the ActionDispatcher boundary to registered ActionSpec.kwargs before executor invocation. Stripped input keys, timeouts, TypeError call-boundary failures, and executor exceptions are reported through structured ActionResult diagnostics. Direct and dry_run host calls keep existing unfiltered behavior.
action_runtime.native_tool_call_empty_diagnostic: When native_tool_calls planning produces no executable tool_calls, ActionFlow returns a skipped ActionResult with diagnostic code action_runtime.native_tool_calls.empty instead of silently reporting an empty evidence stream.
action_runtime.action_record_summary: agent.action.summarize_records(records, validation_command_markers=...) returns authoritative host-facing counts, failed actions, commands attempted/run, and latest_validation where the latest matching validation command wins.

configuration.default_output_format: Omitted .output(..., format=...) reads prompt.default_output_format from the current settings chain; the global default is json, while explicit format="auto" and per-agent/request prompt.default_output_format="auto" remain available for schema-driven selection.
configuration.tuple_ensure_value_contract: Tuple ensure=True flags and runtime ensure_keys require path/key presence only: missing paths fail and share the normal retry budget, while None, blank strings, False, 0, empty lists, and other intentionally empty values remain valid when the path exists.
configuration.tuple_not_null_value_contract: Tuple ensure marker "not_null" requires the path/key to exist and resolve to a meaningful value: None, blank strings, empty lists or wildcard matches, and lists containing missing required values fail and share the normal retry budget; False and 0 remain valid typed values.

triggerflow.runtime_integrity_contract: TriggerFlow preserves execution-local runtime scope across framework-owned fan-out and chunk-internal emits; batch creates a real fan-out signal and isolates concurrent invocations; chunk-emitted signals carry parent signal identity and aggregation scope for DAG-friendly joins; flow.when([...], mode="and") is the event-list shorthand for multi-dependency joins; empty for_each emits an empty collected result; no-hit match restores its layer before continuing; pause_for(..., resume_to="self") records resume_count and defaults to max_resumes=1; pause_for(..., channel_id=..., provider_id=..., wait_mode=..., hot_wait_timeout=..., cold_persistence_policy=..., request_payload_schema=..., response_payload_schema=..., audit_metadata=...) records interrupt planned, persisted, and exposed phases plus a parameterized ExternalWait request envelope; runtime resource key execution_exchange_provider binds a host/plugin ExecutionExchangeProvider whose publish_request(...) can add exchange_id, audit_metadata, and provider_metadata after the interrupt is persisted and before it is exposed; provider publish failures mark the ExternalWait dispatch_state as exposure_failed and emit triggerflow.interrupt_exposure_failed; continue_with(..., resume_request_id=...) records resume accepted, dispatched, and completed/dispatch-failed phases, fails fast before acceptance when callbacks reach expired execution-local leases, and updates the ExternalWait dispatch state for idempotent callback replay; execution.save() includes a versioned top-level execution snapshot with durable TriggerFlow system progress, flow definition fingerprint validation, interrupt/resume ledger, ExternalWait request state, typed recovery diagnostics, resource requirements, lease metadata, TaskDAG graph fingerprint metadata, compaction segment metadata, retained lineage anchors, snapshot artifact refs, load read bounds, and managed execution resource requirements for restart-safe load without serializing live resources; Agently.create_trigger_flow(...) mirrors TriggerFlow(...) for factory-style flow definition creation; flow.create_execution(workspace=None) binds the current session/script default Workspace through runtime resource key workspace and assigns an execution-scoped file root under files/lineage/<root-kind>/<root-id>/.../execution/<execution-id>/files, workspace=False opts out, and workspace=shared_workspace/path/backend binds an explicitly selected Workspace through the execution-local runtime resource key workspace; flow/execution declare_resource_requirement(..., resolver=..., provider_kind=..., config_ref=..., secret_ref=..., fail_policy=...) persists importable resource resolver descriptors; execution.set_compaction_policy(...) forms the snapshot compaction surface; execution.inspect_load(...) reports missing resolver, unhealthy, policy-forbidden, pending resolver, unresolved resource, expired lease warnings, active lease owner conflicts, DAG join state mismatches, lineage-anchor mismatch, missing artifact, and invalid load-read-limit diagnostics; fail-closed pending resolvers or pending managed execution resources report status pending_resources and ready=false until async_load(...) resolves and validates live resources; execution.async_load(...) can rebuild live resources through importable resolvers before graph continuation; execution.inspect_runtime_event_records(...), execution.project_runtime_event_record(...), execution.async_save(..., expected_state_version=...), and idempotent continue_with(..., resume_request_id=...) form the load-facing contract; passing runtime_resources={"workspace": workspace} or runtime_resources={"durable_provider": provider} still binds compatible snapshot and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically, but runtime_resources is only the process-local mount point for live objects already created, restored, and validated by the host; Workspace-backed snapshots are read through the provider and loaded by TriggerFlow for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress; configured RuntimeEvent stores receive the same canonical RuntimeEvent identities that EventCenter dispatches plus durable projection fields for state_version, parent signal, aggregation scope, operator id, interrupt id, resume request id, actor id, exchange id, lease owner id, snapshot refs, and artifact refs; execution.async_save(..., require_distributed_provider=True) fails closed unless providers report CAS, lease, range-read, retention, and event-sequence capabilities and expose matching get_snapshot, lease, and artifact-ref methods; examples/trigger_flow/fastapi_sqlite_exchange_provider.py demonstrates a module-level TriggerFlow object with top-level .to(...) / .when(...) wiring, SQLite snapshot storage, a SQLite ExecutionExchangeProvider, and a FastAPI service boundary without claiming complete production distributed recovery; async_close runs once, clears transient aggregation scratch state, and removes closed executions from the live registry; execution concurrency is a global handler dispatch limit with permit yielding around awaited internal dispatch.

workspace.durable_provider_contract: Workspace remains the public durable information boundary and now exposes typed provider seams for stable reference envelopes, bounded reads, stream reads, RuntimeEvent storage, file-policy metadata, evidence links, retention anchors, checkpoint lookup, checkpoint CAS through put_checkpoint(..., expected_state_version=...), lease claim/heartbeat/release, artifact refs, backend-provider registration, optional scoped prune, and backend capability reporting. RuntimeEvent storage supports idempotency keys, expected_sequence fail-closed append checks, state_version, parent event and causation ids, parent signal id, aggregation scope, operator id, interrupt id, resume request id, actor id, lease owner id, snapshot refs, artifact refs, created_at, and persisted_at. Default Agents expose agent.workspace as a lazy Workspace foundation capability, and default TriggerFlow executions bind the current session/script default Workspace through runtime resource key workspace; the local filesystem plus SQLite backend materializes only when Workspace storage, checkpoint, evidence, read, or file-area behavior is actually used. Agent, execution, and task records carry logical scope partitions while file actions use lineage-scoped files/lineage/<root-kind>/<root-id>/.../<leaf-kind>/<leaf-id>/files roots. The local backend uses WAL, busy timeout, scope indexes, and scoped prune support for local SQLite stability. Application-owned shared Workspaces are created through Workspace(...) or Agently.create_workspace(...) and explicitly bound to Agents, TriggerFlow executions, or service workers when they must share task information; separate Workspaces exchange information only through application-level search/read/write/link logic. Third-party Workspace backends can be passed directly to agent.use_workspace(backend), flow.create_execution(workspace=backend), or registered through Agently.workspace.register_backend_provider(name, factory) and selected with agent.use_workspace(root, provider=name, provider_options={...}); unregistered providers fail fast. TriggerFlow can bind compatible Workspace or durable-provider runtime resources to snapshot and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically; provider snapshots can be read back and passed to TriggerFlow load for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress. A protocol-level remote audit provider proof covers custom WorkspaceBackend replacement across Agent binding, provider registration, TriggerFlow snapshot/RuntimeEvent ports, distributed capability and method checks, lease methods, artifact refs, and AgentExecution evidence links. AgentExecution.async_record_workspace(...) writes requested checkpoints through the Workspace checkpoint-store port and records evidence links between AgentExecution records and checkpoints. The AgentTask task strategy writes its planning, observation, verification, checkpoint, and evidence-link records through Workspace provider ports while keeping strategy decisions in AgentExecution/AgentTask. Workspace does not own TriggerFlow execution policy, AgentExecution strategy selection, approval decisions, DAG readiness, DevTools projection truth, live resource serialization, or cross-space messaging/replication.
workspace.file_io_handler_contract: Workspace exposes WorkspaceFileIOHandler as the public plugin seam for file read/write/export behavior. Workspace owns path containment, deterministic file info, handler dispatch, sha256/file refs, and structured diagnostics; handlers own format-specific parsing, rendering, optional dependencies, MCP-backed adapters, and image preparation. Built-in text read/write is default; PDF, Office, image/VLM preparation, and HTML/Markdown export are optional fail-closed handlers. enable_workspace_file_actions(...) delegates to the bound Workspace when roots match, keeps isolated-root compatibility through the same handler registry, does not overwrite user Actions, and registers export_file only with export=True and write=True. enable_coding_agent_actions(...) is the coding-agent profile over the same Workspace file boundary and adds glob/grep search, targeted edit, unified-diff apply_patch, and stale-guarded write behavior without moving file semantics into shell.
deferred: production external-provider distributed recovery operational guarantees
resumable AgentExecution strategies beyond the current AgentTask task-strategy evidence slice

agent_execution_request_scope.contract: Non-always Agent quick prompt calls create an isolated AgentExecution draft; AgentExecution owns execution-local ModelRequest prompt state and returns AgentExecutionResult. Prompt config .execution is the current one-run prompt section. AgentTurn, Agent.create_turn(...), Agent.set_turn_prompt(...), Agent.set_request_prompt(...), and AgentExecution.set_turn_prompt(...) are removed from the 4.1.3.7 development line. Agent-level persistent state remains on define(...), always=True, set_agent_prompt(...), and stable setup APIs.
agent_execution_request_scope.compatibility_policy: Expression-local chaining is the recommended execution-scoped shape. Multi-statement setup should capture execution = agent.create_execution() or use agent.input(...). Removed AgentTurn and turn/request prompt aliases are not compatibility surfaces for 4.1.3.7. Explicit low-level agent.create_request()/agent.request builders remain available for direct ModelRequest use.

response_stream_type_surface.contract: Common generator and handler type annotations are exposed through agently and agently.types.data. Result-named aliases are the recommended root-level public names: delta yields str, instant/streaming_parse yields StreamingData with is_complete, specific/all yield event-tagged result messages, AgentExecution streams yield AgentExecutionStreamData, and direct Skills stream handlers receive SkillRuntimeStreamItem dictionaries. Response-named aliases are compatibility-only aliases under agently.types.data and are not re-exported from the agently root.
response_stream_type_surface.compatibility_policy: StreamingData.is_complete remains the canonical instant/streaming_parse completion field. Response-named type aliases remain compatibility-only aliases in agently.types.data and are deprecated in favor of Result-named aliases; they will be removed in Agently 4.2.

model_request_result_facade.contract: get_result() is the recommended reusable facade for text, data, metadata, and streaming readers. Agent quick prompt chains return AgentExecutionResult; direct ModelRequest calls return ModelRequestResult. get_response() remains a compatibility alias. ModelRequestResult.result points to itself so older response.result reader chains continue to work.
model_request_result_facade.compatibility_policy: Recommended documentation, examples, and Skills guidance use result = agent.input(...).output(...).get_result(); data = result.get_data(). ModelResponseResult is no longer a public facade or compatibility alias; use ModelRequestResult. Direct ModelResponse construction remains deprecated for removal in Agently 4.2.

agent_execution_task_loop.contract: The 4.1.3.7 task-strategy contract remains the published baseline for a narrow single-task, single-Agent-owner strategy carried by AgentExecution. The 4.1.3.8 development target hardens that baseline through framework-level task-execution quality fixes: planner-visible capability summaries, structured bounded-step scope, capability/evidence requirements, verifier guards, and removal of strong-instruction or business-specific special-case fixes from framework paths. agent.goal(goal_or_goals, success_criteria=None), its plural alias agent.goals(...), agent.create_task(...), and agent.create_task_loop(...) return task-strategy AgentExecution drafts, not a separate recommended AgentTask execution owner. The second goal argument is the current success-criteria entrypoint; separate success_criteria(...) and success_standards(...) builders are not current public surfaces. effort(...) accepts preset names or a detailed strategy expansion for budget, planning, execution, verification, replan, progress, and reflection_density; effort budget values such as iteration_limit, model_call_limit, and wall_time_seconds are soft strategy metadata for planning complexity, reflection density, repair posture, and evidence depth, and do not silently set task-strategy max_iterations or AgentExecution hard limits. Hosts that need hard resource controls must use explicit task options or limits={...}. By default, AgentTask does not impose model-request, iteration, TaskBoard tick, or Action round quotas; no-progress and idle timeouts remain liveness guards for stuck executions, not strategy evidence. effort reflection_density maps low to final reflection plus planner-marked important process points, medium to each major task node or TaskBoard card/tick, and high to every framework-observable bounded step, Action/ACP call, TaskBoard card, and final reflection; reflection records are Workspace evidence and verifier/replan input but are not completion evidence by themselves. Strong-format intermediate process requests use Agently .output(..., format=...) with the appropriate format; declared non-JSON parser failures try JSON fallback, and structured control/final output contracts accept fallback only when the parsed value is dict-shaped with diagnostics. Selected high-value internal process requests may include short pre/post process fields such as intent, decision_basis, self_check, short_summary, verification_summary, criterion_checks, repair_summary, and progress_message; these fields are added manually only where a downstream consumer exists, are stored as process_summary, are not a public runtime mode, and are not grounding or completion evidence. Task execution_strategy defaults to auto. In auto, AgentTask runs one task_shape_analysis ModelRequest that first gives natural-language task-shape analysis and then a thin structured execution_hint with an optional minimal initial_taskboard_plan; strategy policy resolves effective_execution_strategy to flat or taskboard, and small linear auto-selected boards can fall back to flat before TaskBoard lifecycle execution. Explicit execution flat/taskboard or AgentExecution.strategy("flat"|"taskboard") beats the hint, and nested AgentExecution instances inherit parent strategy context unless explicitly overridden. TaskBoard remains an execution substrate after strategy selection and does not classify task complexity. Scoped retrieval returns factual locator_ref/evidence_snippet records with truncated bounded-snippet facts, and model-hot retrieval, available-readback, Action-artifact-readback, Workspace-readback, and verifier artifact-ref views are derived from the canonical EvidenceEnvelope.evidence_items ledger while omitting reconstructable SHA/bytes/backend provenance; raw Workspace/Blocks evidence keeps audit metadata. Evidence items preserve id, kind, status(ok|failed|empty), body_state(full|bounded|truncated|ref_only), provenance, view-local cite_as handles, and producer-declared structural aliases when available; failed/empty items support unavailable or missing-data claims only, ref_only items support discovery/ref-pointer claims only, and structured evidence_use bindings let deterministic guards reconcile visible aliases to canonical ids and reject ambiguous ids, unresolved ids, or impossible support before model verification without business-specific action-name rules. content/content_preview/text/excerpt/snippet fields are bounded previews for visible content only, TaskBoard final verification carries board source_refs with preserved content_state boundaries, TaskBoard Workspace target_refs become bounded readback cards, external URL target_refs remain Action evidence work, and blocked scoped-retrieval cards without explicit next action synthesize expanded evidence and continuation cards so downstream consumers, not terminal-only verifiers, decide whether intermediate evidence is now enough. Failed terminal Flat verification carries compact repair_context into the next Flat work unit and Workspace artifact draft request when present, including acceptance deltas, repair constraints, next-step requirements, and exact evidence anchors without restoring cold provenance to model-hot prompts. TaskDAG is no longer an AgentTask bounded-step strategy; if a planner or legacy config proposes dynamic_task/execution_dag or effort execution.step_plan='dag', AgentTask degrades the step to direct execution with diagnostics. ACP is a first-class Action plus ExecutionResource(kind="acp"); planner/user direct ACP use and recovery fallback after bounded-step or TaskBoard-card retry exhaustion use the same capability/evidence contract, and ACP is not a route that bypasses AgentExecution or AgentTask policy. acp_list_agents returns non-binding common adapter-name hints for codex, claude code/cc, openclaw, hermes/hermes agent, and gemini; these hints are not runnable-agent evidence. DynamicTask is the current compatibility/convenience facade over DAG planning and execution for direct independent TaskDAG/DynamicTask use, not the strategic task lifecycle, task-strategy router, or AgentExecution route. Agent.use_dynamic_task(...) and AgentExecution.use_dynamic_task(...) fail fast with migration diagnostics; use Agently.create_dynamic_task(...) or direct TaskDAGExecutor(...) for independent DAG workflows. Consumers read final data, text, streams, metadata, execution_strategy, effective_execution_strategy, task_shape_analysis, reflection refs, Workspace refs, and task refs through AgentExecutionResult or the execution stream/meta facade. get_async_generator(type="delta") returns the public text-increment stream, including model-generated text increments plus projected process paragraphs for progress, structured progress_message, action observations, snapshots, heartbeats, phases, retry markers, and terminal task results; get_async_generator(type="instant") returns structured stream items. Non-progress process fields remain available in instant/records but are not projected as public text delta; action/progress paragraphs remain observation text, not completion evidence. Artifact writers that choose plain delta for long natural-language bodies must treat <$retry>...</$retry> as a public replay boundary at the consumer side instead of forcing the body through .output() only to obtain instant fields. Workspace artifact deliveries are accepted only after trusted Workspace readback records path, bytes, sha256, bounded preview, file_refs, and acceptance-locator ledger evidence in cold evidence; model-hot verifier input carries path/ref handles, bounded content or preview, truncation status, and locator-first targeted readback snippets while integrity metadata stays programmatically traceable; capability_evidence.artifacts.readback carries trusted readback path handles, not path#sha ids, and write-success/readback-failure paths diagnose agent_task.workspace_artifact.readback_failed or agent_task.workspace_artifact.readback_insufficient instead of budget or iteration shortfall. Completion acceptance requires model-owned verification plus conservative host guards; completed means accepted=true and artifact_status=accepted, blocked means accepted=false and artifact_status=blocked, while explicitly configured max_iterations can return accepted=false and artifact_status=partial. The task strategy writes Workspace checkpoints through the checkpoint-store port and records task evidence relationships with Workspace evidence links. agent.resume(task_id) and agent.async_resume(task_id) rebuild resumable AgentTask snapshots as task-strategy AgentExecution drafts; AgentExecutionResult resume delegates to the same Agent facade when task_refs carry a resumable task id. Agent.resume_task and Agent.async_resume_task are compatibility aliases only and should not be promoted as the recommended lifecycle.
agent_execution_task_loop.artifact_stream_contract: Long or prose-heavy Workspace artifact bodies may use natural text streams without .output(); retry/reset uses structured $status when available, and public <$retry>...</$retry> is only a plain-delta consumer replay boundary. Long trusted artifact verification may include acceptance-locator ledger items plus bounded targeted_readbacks from locator line/offset anchors; required-section and source/risk/reference/coverage anchors remain fallback.

current_slice: explicitly enabled Actions, Skills, or Skill packs as AgentTask step capabilities
planner-visible capability summaries instead of provider- or example-specific prompt patches
structured bounded-step capability scope instead of prose-only step instructions
Agently .output(..., format=...) control for strong-format intermediate requests with JSON dict fallback diagnostics
model-owned planning, verification, and replan decisions
host guards for missing criteria, risky action evidence, approval-required actions, missing final deliverables, and structured capability/evidence requirements
structured task refs, diagnostics, progress/snapshot stream items, result facade consumption, and provider-backed Workspace evidence links
deferred: distributed pause/resume beyond the single-task agent.resume(...) snapshot slice

agent_execution_task_loop.compatibility_policy: New documentation and examples should keep AgentExecutionResult as the common consumption surface and use agent.resume(task_id) / await agent.async_resume(task_id) for task-strategy recovery. Task-style compatibility readers and resume_task aliases may remain callable, but they should not be promoted as a second recommended public lifecycle. Future AgentTask strategy hardening must strengthen the task strategy behind AgentExecution without changing this public ownership boundary.

image.attachment_contract: Builds text plus image_url rich-content blocks and keeps attachment as the low-level passthrough.
